title: ruscus humilis
intro: Ruscus humilis, also known as the Dwarf Ruscus or Dwarf Butcher's Broom, is a low-growing, evergreen perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The health benefits of Ruscus humilis include reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, which can aid in the healing process and prevent scarring. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to support skin health and wound healing.
